synopsis: The book gives a compressed mathematical introduction to probability, statistical inference, and learning for readers with calculus and basic linear algebra. It moves from random variables, expectation, convergence, and inequalities through estimation, bootstrapping, hypothesis testing, Bayesian methods, regression, multivariate models, independence, causal inference, nonparametric estimation, and classification.

Tom Barren grows up in a technologically perfected version of 2016, but after a personal loss he misuses a time machine and becomes stranded in the flawed world we recognize. As he encounters different versions of his family, career, and romantic life, he must decide which timeline constitutes the future he wants.

In a corporate-controlled spacefaring future, a scientific expedition is guarded by a self-aware security unit that has secretly disabled its governor module and calls itself Murderbot. When a neighboring mission goes silent, the unit must investigate the danger and protect its human clients while concealing its autonomy and working out what it wants.

The book traces a business career built on seeking overlooked opportunities, acting on supply-and-demand imbalances, and investing in distressed real estate and unfashionable industries. Through deals, setbacks, and the Tribune Company bankruptcy, it explains a contrarian approach to risk, independent judgment, organizational culture, and long-term value.

The book traces Amazon's creation and rapid rise under Jeff Bezos, from its origins as an online bookseller to a defining company of early internet commerce. It examines the business model, financing, customer-service discipline, expansion strategy, internal culture, and pressure to grow quickly while confronting the company's uncertain path to profitability.

sources.title: Amazon.com: Get Big Fast - Google Books

synopsis: After taking a nine-dollar-an-hour job as a guard at a private prison in Louisiana, an investigative reporter documents understaffing, neglect, violence, and the ways the work changes guards. His account is interwoven with a history of for-profit incarceration, tracing its roots from slavery and convict leasing through the modern private-prison industry.

This biography follows physicist Robert Oppenheimer from his early scientific career through his leadership of the Los Alamos laboratory that built the atomic bomb. It examines his response to nuclear weapons, role in Cold War policy debates, opposition to the hydrogen bomb, and political destruction during the 1954 security hearing.

The book presents a method for accelerating organizational performance by raising standards, increasing the pace of work, and concentrating resources on the mission. It explains how leaders can align people around a few priorities, make difficult tradeoffs, challenge incrementalism, build an accountable culture, and execute with urgency and intensity without first redesigning the business.

A fictional Seeker visits a Library of Wisdom to learn how to make better decisions and help his children avoid repeating his mistakes. Through conversations with the Librarian, Warren Buffett, and Charlie Munger, he studies inversion, common errors, and the value of simplicity, sound judgment, and avoiding preventable trouble.

sources.title: Summary of All I Want to Know is Where I'm Going to Die
